tasks.title: Documentation guards and final Phase 9 gates
tasks.definition_of_done: Docs guard tests in crates/opi-coding-agent/tests/productized_packages_docs.rs assert the Phase 9 evidence baseline, current .repo/pi-0.80.2 baseline, English/Chinese synchronization, Phase 9-14 roadmap consistency, Phase 10 Models/Auth and AgentHarness targets, future ecosystem entry conditions, and forbidden current-scope overclaims; the final guard task owns only the docs guard test file and relies on prior documentation tasks for document edits; final verification proves no production Rust source, Cargo manifest, runtime script, provider auth, image generation, custom UI protocol, package ecosystem feature, web/share flow, pi session compatibility path, or opi-types crate was added by the guard task; final fmt, clippy, doc, workspace tests, and smoke gates pass.

inference_notes.reason: Derived from the Phase 9 Testing and Guard Strategy and Success Criteria 1-9; this task closes the product acceptance scenarios after the documentation substrate tasks land.
Promoted from documentation to workspace only because Phase 9 requires Rust integration docs-guard tests; task-owned paths exclude production Rust source and Cargo manifests.

Phase 9 Non-Goals forbid code migration, CodingHarness relocation, Models/Auth implementation, provider OAuth/subscription login, image generation, custom extension UI protocol, npm registry/gallery/update/enable/disable, browser/web UI product, session sharing service, pi session compatibility promise, and a shared opi-types crate.

DoD clarified that final guard verification validates prior docs tasks but does not own their document edits.

acceptance_scenarios.source: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-24-phase9-pi-0-80-2-baseline-realignment-design.md#Phase 9 Success Criteria 1,5; docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-24-phase9-pi-0-80-2-baseline-realignment-design.md#Evidence Baseline in the Alignment Matrix
acceptance_scenarios.scenario: A maintainer can open the English and Chinese alignment matrices and see the 0.80.2 document control, architecture analysis, local evidence anchors, three-layer dashboard, and opi-agent Partial status caused by the generic harness gap.

Current normative docs no longer describe .repo/pi-0.75.3 as the studied upstream baseline; .repo/pi-0.75.3 may appear only as historical prior-baseline context, while current-baseline statements name .repo/pi-0.80.2.
English and Chinese normative docs carry equivalent baseline, roadmap, alignment dashboard, future ecosystem, and non-goal statements for every Phase 9 documentation surface.

The roadmap consistently lists Phase 9 baseline realignment, Phase 10 core architecture deepening, Phase 11 tooling quality, Phase 12 provider correctness, Phase 13 session tree/context reconstruction, and Phase 14 TUI product polish; Models/Auth and AgentHarness are named as Phase 10 targets.

Future ecosystem candidates such as provider OAuth, broad provider catalog, image generation, custom extension UI, npm/gallery/update, web/share, provider hooks, and pi session migration are documented with entry conditions, not as scheduled near-term phase promises.
Docs guard tests reject current-scope claims for OAuth parity, image generation, custom extension UI parity, npm/gallery/update/enable/disable, web/share, and pi session compatibility.

The Phase 9 task diff changes only documentation and docs guard tests; it does not change production Rust source, Cargo manifests, runtime scripts, provider auth, image generation, custom UI, package ecosystem behavior, web/share behavior, pi session compatibility, or add an opi-types crate.

Verdict ARCHIVE-READY.
